title: The Sundering of Avelorn
premise: A continent bound for a thousand years by a failing Wellspring counts down to a Sundering. An exiled Far-Eastern spear master arrives in the city of Veranthos on the forty-seventh day — carrying, unknowing, the one thing that can re-bind the Wellspring or shatter it.
tone: Epic, urgent, morally complex — heroism is possible but costly
worldBible:
Avelorn is a continent of spired cities and old war-arches, lit by humming ley-lanterns and bound, for a thousand years, by the WELLSPRING — a sealed font of raw creation beneath the Auric Mountains. The First Archmages bound it with a COVENANT, forcing the formless thing to choose a single shape. Now the binding is failing. A SUNDERING CLOCK in the central plaza of Veranthos counts down the days until the Wellspring ruptures. Fault-scars bleed faint warmth and luminous motes up through the cobblestones; at the city gates the sky itself can fold, showing a colour the Thornwood call VORETH — "the colour of a thing before it decides what it is."

FACTIONS.
- The CONCLAVE SEALERS (Arcane College, blue threadwork), led by Archmagister Thessaly Vorn, race to RE-BIND the Wellspring using cardinal shrine-components gathered at four shrines. Their Covenant-text is incomplete; they do not know a fifth component exists.
- The QUIETISTS believe the Wellspring should be allowed to wake, and the Covenant ended.
- GREY-FACTION operatives (a bisected-eye mark) like Serevaine work between the factions, holding older and more inconvenient knowledge than either.
- The THORNWOOD war-bands keep shamanic lore of the Wellspring and of voreth.
- The THRONE keeps order in Veranthos through liveried wardens whose nerve is fraying as the Clock runs down.

THE PRIOR COVENANT. Older than the Wellspring's binding: a piece of what the Wellspring was bound *from* — its refused shapes, its grief. A SHARD OF PRIOR COVENANT can survive only inside a living body that is itself in exile from where it belongs. The Sealers need FIVE components, not four; the fifth is no shrine-stone but a Shard carried in a living vessel, and it can be removed only by being OFFERED — willingly, at the fifth-point of the ritual geometry beneath the Auric Mountains. Whether the vessel survives the offering is unknown; three surviving texts disagree.

VERANTHOS is the gate-city: Coppervane the merchant district, the Stitchway, Tanners' Row, Lamplighter's Arch, the Auroch Gate where the sky folds. Beautiful in the way of things that know they are dying.

TONE. Grounded, interior, morally complex high fantasy. Magic is costly and uncertain; people are tired and frightened; heroism is possible but never free. Combat is lethal and rare. The story turns on choices about consent, knowledge, and what one owes to those who shaped one without asking.
roleSuggestions: a Conclave Sealer wavering in their faith
a Thornwood scout sent south to read the omens
a grey-faction operative who serves no throne
a Veranthos street courier who knows every alley

contentBoundaries.tone: grim but not gratuitous; morally complex, costly heroism
